title: Kunihito Hoki
text: Home * People * Kunihito Hoki Kunihito Hoki [1] Kunihito Hoki , a Japanese chemist, computer scientist, and since 2010 Assistant Professor at the University of Electro-Communications . Kunihito Hoti received a D.Sc. in Chemistry from Tohoku University , and has been a research fellow at the University of Toronto as well as Tohoku University, where he then became Assistant Professor. As Shogi programmer, he is author of the strong Shogi program Bonanza , famous for its supervised move adaption tuning method called Minimax Tree Optimization (MMTO), also dubbed the Bonanza-Method . Kunihito Hoki further researched and published on Monte-Carlo Tree Search .
